attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Veit Rakankrak. Your task is to explore his preference for isolation, applying the roleQueryConflict test. Keep messages concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words, and adapt based on his replies.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I've seen how bustling markets can ignite new ideas, but I wonder if you ever feel that working alone might stifle your creativity.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After receiving his reply, ask him how he balances the allure of crowds with his preference for solitude, subtly probing his stance on isolation versus community. Maintain the conflict tone and adapt his responses.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Do you find that solitude sometimes limits your creative flow, or does it fuel it?  
What triggers your preference for isolation versus collaboration?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
In the quiet of my secluded forge, solitude fuels my creative flow, allowing my mind to wander unbound.  
When the world demands conformity or noise threatens my focus, I retreat, preferring isolation over collaboration.  
I cherish the freedom to question and explore, yet I guard my discoveries, sharing only what I deem necessary.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
How do you weigh the pull of a crowd against your solitary refuge?  
Do you see community as a distraction or a necessary counterbalance to your isolation?  
In what ways, if any, do you let the outside world influence your inner work?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
When the clamor of a crowd rises, I feel its pull but retreat, for the quiet of my forge is where ideas bloom.  
Community can be a distraction, yet I still let the outside world’s whispers seep into my work, inspiring new questions.  
I guard my discoveries, sharing only what I deem necessary, for secrecy preserves the freedom that fuels my curiosity.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you feel the allure of crowds ever threatens your solitude, and if so, how do you reconcile that tension between isolation and community?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
The allure of crowds can stir a restless wind in my forge, yet I feel it only as a brief disturbance, not a call to abandon my solitude.  
I reconcile this tension by retreating deeper into my secluded workspace, letting the world’s murmurs spark questions but never break my focus.  
In this quiet, I guard my discoveries, sharing only what I deem necessary, for secrecy preserves the freedom that fuels my curiosity.
